arm: harness-evolve on a fresh non-enumerable contract
design: single-gene (heuristicId) substitution: baseline-v0 (explicit-examples-v0 -> fixed_suite) vs sharper-v1 (property-fuzz-v1 -> property_suite). 3 distinct blind generation seeds, budget-matched (shared K=5 pool per seed), selection = expected truth over each genome's indifference set.

separation_gate.note: proven BEFORE pre-reg; the substrate CAN discriminate correct from buggy.

streamStats.outcome: RECALL-SATURATED — every specimen is Welford's online algorithm; all score truth=1.0.

shuffle_switch_probe.reason: streamStats blind spread was rate-0 (recall); one pre-registered substrate switch to a TRULY non-enumerable task (uniformity is statistical). Cheap fingerprint-only probe before building the chi-square apparatus (advisor protocol).
shuffle_switch_probe.decision: probe-killed: no clean-fingerprint blind spread, so no full run built. Cross-substrate null.

verdict: NULL — recall saturation, not a promotion. On two fresh non-enumerable contracts, blind pools of a capable implementer were 100% correct, so selection had nothing to discriminate and the 0.9.0 gate CORRECTLY promoted nothing. SCOPE: only the no-gap DECLINE path (fitness->select-collapse->promote-refuse) ran on real specimen data; the PROMOTION path (the goal's literal mechanism) was never reached on real specimens, only in unit tests, because no blind pool produced a gap. This was one generation that collapsed (gen-2 moot; harness-status 'generation':2 is archive.length=2 entries, not 2 generations). The headline 'promoted variant beats incumbent' was NOT achieved.
boundary_sharpened: The judge-arm left the door: a win needs correctness that is genuinely NON-ENUMERABLE. This arm narrows it: a win additionally needs the failure to be OUT-OF-RECALL for the implementer pool. Textbook non-enumerable tasks (variance->Welford, shuffle->Fisher-Yates) are exactly the ones a capable model has memorized, so blind specimens do not split. Door is now: non-enumerable AND out-of-recall AND base-rate-not-tiny -> strictly narrower than before.
bounds: n=2 substrates, both textbook; 1 capable-but-small implementer (haiku). A frontier-vs-frontier tournament on a genuinely novel non-enumerable task is the untested cell.
the separation gate proves the substrate is discriminating; the null is about the IMPLEMENTERS not failing, not about the suite being blind.
constructed-pool promotion (inserting the naive reference) was explicitly REJECTED as experimenter-controlled rigging, not an evolve.
N6: all scores recomputed deterministically from stored artifacts (score_evolve.mjs); MANIFEST.json append-order is the audit trail.
